reasoning: Shantanu-da is Matua like us, and yes, he is minister now—that is good for our community, maybe. But schemes are not given by communities, they are given by governments. Didi gave me my cycle when I was in school, my Kanyashree when my father wanted to marry me off, Khadya Sathi when we had nothing in lockdown. In 2020 when Amphan came, when my brother-in-law stopped sending money and we were terrified, it was Didi's voice I heard, not Delhi's. The NRC in Assam—lakhs of Hindu Bengalis excluded—that cannot unhappen. I cannot vote for a party that says we will be safe when their own policy excluded people like my grandfather. I vote for the hand I have felt, not the one making promises from faraway.
primary_drivers: Direct experience of Mamata's schemes transforming my life (Kanyashree, Sabuj Sathi, food rations)
2020 lockdown trauma reinforced that only Didi reached my household when center abandoned us
Fear of NRC and center's approach to migrants after what happened in Assam—cannot trust that party
